Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Complex A sophisticated cyber attack targeting water and wastewater systems involves multiple stages, starting with reconnaissance to identify potential entry points, followed by resource development to establish command and control infrastructure. Initial access is gained through exploiting public-facing applications, leading to execution of malicious scripts. Persistence is maintained via web shells, while privilege escalation allows adversaries to gain full system control. Defense evasion includes disabling security tools, credential access is attempted through brute force attacks, and discovery of the network configuration aids lateral movement. Collection of sensitive data precedes exfiltration over alternative protocols, culminating in impact through inhibiting system recovery, potentially deploying ransomware like AvosLocker.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Network Traffic, Command & Control Server, Target System Artifact, Public IP/Network Block, User Account, File System, Network Traffic, DNS Record, Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method, Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), Response Metadata (Mechanism), Bash Script, Web Shell, Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism, Disable or Modify Tools, User Account Authentication (Mechanism), System Network Configuration Discovery, Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), File Access (Mechanism), Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol, Process Creation (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 309 Name: Network Topology Mapping
stage.description: The attackers conduct active scanning to map out the network topology of the water and wastewater systems, identifying potential entry points.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 522 Name: Malicious Hardware Component Replacement
stage.description: Adversaries acquire and set up command and control servers to support future phases of the attack.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 121 Name: Exploit Non-Production Interfaces
stage.description: Attackers exploit an exposed server to gain initial access to the network, mirroring past methods used by C0018.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 88 Name: OS Command Injection
stage.description: Malicious scripts are deployed via command-line interfaces to execute initial payloads.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 650 Name: Upload a Web Shell to a Web Server
stage.description: A web shell is uploaded to maintain persistent access to the compromised system.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Attackers escalate privileges to gain complete control over the targeted system.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 578 Name: Disable Security Software
stage.description: Security tools are disabled to evade detection and hinder incident response efforts.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 49 Name: Password Brute Forcing
stage.description: Repeated attempts are made to guess user credentials, aiming to gain unauthorized access.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 309 Name: Network Topology Mapping
stage.description: Adversaries gather detailed knowledge about the network layout to plan further lateral movements.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 324 Name: TCP (ISN) Sequence Predictability Probe
stage.description: Attackers move laterally across the network using remote services like RDP or SSH.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 328 Name: TCP 'RST' Flag Checksum Probe
stage.description: Sensitive data is collected from local systems prior to exfiltration.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 590 Name: IP Address Blocking
stage.description: Data is transferred using non-standard protocols like DNS tunneling to avoid detection.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Ransomware like AvosLocker is deployed to encrypt critical system files, rendering them unusable and demanding a ransom for decryption keys.

Scenario.summary: The complex cyber attack on water and wastewater systems by the C0018 campaign involves extensive preparation and execution phases. Starting with reconnaissance and resource development, the attackers gain initial access through vulnerable public-facing applications, ensuring persistence and escalating privileges. They employ defense evasion techniques to remain undetected while attempting to collect credentials and map the network. Lateral movement follows, culminating in data exfiltration over alternative channels. Finally, the impact phase sees the deployment of ransomware, severely disrupting operations and demanding a ransom.
